# Introduction

In this chapter, we will begin developing a vertical "Subway Surfer" style game using **Kontra.js**. We will focus on two core pillars of game feel: a scrolling infinite background and a lane-based movement system with input buffering (cooldowns).

::: {.callout-note}
Kontra.js is a lightweight gaming micro-library. It is perfect for this project because it handles the game loop and sprite management without the overhead of larger engines.
:::

# Step 1: Set up HTML and Kontra

First, we need to establish our boilerplate. This includes a `<canvas>` element where the game will be rendered and the inclusion of the Kontra library via CDN.

```javascript
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Subway Game</title>
    <script src="[https://unpkg.com/kontra/kontra.min.js](https://unpkg.com/kontra/kontra.min.js)"></script>
    <style>
        canvas {
            display: block;
            margin: auto;
            background-color: #88ccee;
            border: 3px solid black;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <canvas id="game" width="600" height="800"></canvas>
    <script>
        let {init, Sprite, GameLoop, initKeys, keyPressed, load, Text} = kontra;
        let {canvas, context} = init('game');
        initKeys();
    </script>
</body>
</html>
```

# Step 2: Background Sprites & GameLoop

To create a world, we need a background. In Kontra, we load assets using the `load` function. Once the image is ready, we create a `Sprite` and initiate the `GameLoop`.

::: {.callout-tip}
### Asset Management
Always ensure your `assets/` folder is in the same directory as your HTML file, or the image will fail to load.
:::

```javascript
load('assets/background.png').then(images => {
    let [backgroundImage] = images;

    let background = Sprite({
        x: 0,
        y: 0,
        width: canvas.width,
        height: canvas.height,
        image: backgroundImage,
        draw() {
            this.context.drawImage(this.image, this.x, this.y, canvas.width, canvas.height);
        }
    });

    let loop = GameLoop({
        render() {
            background.draw();
        }
    });
    loop.start();
});
```

# Step 3: Creating the Infinite Scroll

## 3.1 Scrolling Logic
To simulate movement, we move the background down the Y-axis. When it leaves the screen, we reset it to the top.



## 3.2 Doubling Up for Continuity
A single background leaves a gap when it resets. To fix this, we create `background2` and stack it directly above the first one.

```javascript
let background2 = Sprite({
    x: 0,
    y: -canvas.height, // Start directly above the canvas
    width: canvas.width,
    height: canvas.height,
    image: backgroundImage,
    update() {
        this.y += 3;
        if (this.y > 0) this.y = -canvas.height; 
    },
    draw() {
        this.context.drawImage(this.image, this.x, this.y, canvas.width, canvas.height);
    }
});
```

# Step 4: Adding the Player

We represent the player as a simple blue square. Note the `lane` property; this is a **custom state** we use to track which of the three lanes the player occupies.

| Lane | Description | Default X |
|------|-------------|-----------|
| 1    | Left        | 176       |
| 2    | Middle      | 281       |
| 3    | Right       | 386       |

```javascript
let player = Sprite({
    x: 281,
    y: 700,
    width: 40,
    height: 40,
    lane: 2,
    color: 'blue'
});
```

# Step 5: Movement and Polishing

## 5.1 Lane Snapping
Instead of free movement, the player "snaps" to specific X coordinates based on their current lane. We use a `switch` statement to map the `lane` integer to a screen position.

## 5.2 The Movement Cooldown
Without a cooldown, the game updates at ~60fps, meaning a single tap of the arrow key would send the player flying across all lanes instantly.

::: {.callout-warning}
### Why use a Cooldown?
A cooldown (or "debounce") ensures that one key press equals exactly one lane change. This is vital for the "snappy" feel expected in runners.
:::

```javascript
// Inside the Player Sprite update()
update() {
    if (this.cooldown > 0) {
        this.cooldown -= 1;
    } else {
        if (keyPressed('arrowleft') && this.lane > 1) {
            this.lane -= 1;
            this.cooldown = 12; // Frames to wait before next move
        }
        if (keyPressed('arrowright') && this.lane < 3) {
            this.lane += 1;
            this.cooldown = 12;
        }

        // Snap X position to the Lane
        switch (this.lane) {
            case 1: this.x = 176; break;
            case 2: this.x = 281; break;
            default: this.x = 386;
        }
    }
}
```
